title: Mapping and Counting High-Intensity Perineuronal Nets in the Somatosensory Cortex
author: Dana Layo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcf8itrw

steps.title: Download ImageJ from the NIH Have...
steps.instruction: Download ImageJ from the NIH Have 10x whole somatosensory cortex images containing the dentate gyrus and striatum (these are sagittal directions) -- Know measured equivalent of a pixel from the microscope using 10x objective Have the images that you will be analyzing downloaded to the computer Have a digital copy of your mouse atlas

steps.title: Calibrate ImageJ to the scale from...
steps.instruction: Calibrate ImageJ to the scale from the objective of the microscope

steps.title: Open image in ImageJ Analyze--> Set...
steps.instruction: Open image in ImageJ Analyze--> Set Scale-->Insert your measurement values from the microscope--> check Global--> Click out of image Every time you open another image, make sure to uncheck "deactivate global calibration", otherwise you will have to reset the parameters every time you open a new image.

steps.title: Align map to fluorescent image
steps.instruction: Align map to fluorescent image

steps.title: Open corresponding map image in ImageJ
steps.instruction: Open corresponding map image in ImageJ

steps.title: Overlay map onto fluorescent image Image-->...
steps.instruction: Overlay map onto fluorescent image Image--> Overlay-->Add Image--> select appropriate map Image--> Overlay--> To ROI Manager When adding the image, make sure you make it ~20% opaque so you are able to see the image beneath it

steps.title: Make the map fit to the...
steps.instruction: Make the map fit to the cortex of the fluorescent image. To rotate the fluorescent image to the map, deselect the map then go to Image--> Rotate... and insert how many degrees you would like to rotate this image. Reselect the map and drag to fit again. End goal: The curve of the striatum and the front edge of the hippocampus will line up. The front of the tissue should line up with the map as best as possible.

steps.title: Flatten this overlay into one picture
steps.instruction: Flatten this overlay into one picture: Image-->Overlay-->Flatten. To Save: File-->Save As (tiff)--> select the folder with the OG image --> name this image differently (we suggest adding "flatten (degree of rotation)" to the end of it) Keep this flattened image open in ImageJ

steps.title: Increase image brightness so you can...
steps.instruction: Increase image brightness so you can see the border of the striatum and hippocampus Image--> adjust--> brightness/contrast (slide the contrast tool)

steps.title: Select the polygon tool and outline the whole somatosensory cortex
steps.instruction: Select the polygon tool and outline the whole somatosensory cortex. Double click on the final point to finish and connect the outline.

steps.title: Press 't' on the keyboard to...
steps.instruction: Press 't' on the keyboard to save the outline to the ROI Manager

steps.title: Rename the ROI
steps.instruction: Rename the ROI: In ROI manager, select ROI and then the 'Rename' function. Name SS1 Area.

steps.title: To acquire the area
steps.instruction: To acquire the area: In ROI Manager, select Measure and record the area value

steps.title: To Save
steps.instruction: To Save: in ROI Manager, go to 'More'--> 'Save'--> then save in the same folder that you have for the original and flattened image

steps.title: Outline primary somatosensory subregions (the subregions...
steps.instruction: Outline primary somatosensory subregions (the subregions will change depending on what the section is)

steps.title: Increase image brightness so you can...
steps.instruction: Increase image brightness so you can see the border of the striatum and hippocampus Image--> adjust--> brightness/contrast (slide the contrast tool)

steps.title: Select the polygon tool and outline...
steps.instruction: Select the polygon tool and outline ONE subregion at a time Double click on the final point to finish and connect the outline.

steps.title: Press 't' on the keyboard to...
steps.instruction: Press 't' on the keyboard to save the outline to the ROI Manager

steps.title: Rename the ROI
steps.instruction: Rename the ROI: In ROI manager, select ROI and then the 'Rename' function. Name (Subregion) Area. Do this for all subregions in the image. Make sure you rename them as you go. Advice: Make sure to check 'show all' in ROI manager to make sure the borders of the subregions do not overlap. This will help with consistency within one image.

steps.title: Save all of the ROIs as individual files
steps.instruction: Save all of the ROIs as individual files: In ROI Manager, Select ONE ROI file, then go to 'More'--> 'Save'--> then save in the same folder that you have for the original and flattened image Repeat this until all areas are saved

steps.title: To acquire the area
steps.instruction: To acquire the area: In ROI Manager, select Measure and record the area values

steps.title: Set-up
steps.instruction: Set-up: Make sure to calibrate ImageJ! Open original fluorescent image in ImageJ and rotate image per rotation degree in the flattened name (Image--> Transform--> Rotate...)

steps.title: Open original fluorescent image in ImageJ...
steps.instruction: Open original fluorescent image in ImageJ and rotate image per rotation degree in the flattened name (Image--> Transform--> Rotate...)

steps.title: Drag subregion area file into ImageJ,...
steps.instruction: Drag subregion area file into ImageJ, press 't' to open this in ROI Manager

steps.title: Make sure 'show all' is checked in the ROI Manager
steps.instruction: Make sure 'show all' is checked in the ROI Manager

steps.title: Maximize the contrast using the brightness/contrast...
steps.instruction: Maximize the contrast using the brightness/contrast tool Image--> Adjust--> Brightness/Contrast Make sure you know what the numbers are on the Brightness/Contrast tool are when you first maximize the contrast. The more you slide the tool back and forth, those values will change. Advice: It is best to hit 'reset' every couple adjustments in order to keep the contrast accurate.

steps.title: Count all PNNs that are visible...
steps.instruction: Count all PNNs that are visible (look at criteria below) by clicking on it (point tool) and pressing 't' on the keyboard to save it to the ROI Manager. High-Intensity PNN Criteria: PNN must be at least 80% complete* (you can tell by looking at the unaltered photo) *these may be filled-in circles or donut shaped. Both are PNNs, it just depends on sectioning planes

steps.title: To Save PNN Counts
steps.instruction: To Save PNN Counts: In ROI Manager press 'deselect' --> More--> Save (make file name "subregion PNN") make sure this is saved to the same folder as the original image Make sure to press 'deselect' before saving. If you have not done this, none of the counts will be saved.

steps.title: How many PNNs did you count? In ROI Manager
steps.instruction: How many PNNs did you count? In ROI Manager: 'deselect'--> Measure Record the amount of points (that is your count)

steps.title: Repeat for every subregion
steps.instruction: Repeat for every subregion
